title: Security Posture — Attack Paths Fragment
description: LLM-authored data driving the seven numbered attack-path bullets that follow the Security Posture heatmap. Each entry maps one canonical attack class (defined in data/attack-class-taxonomy.yaml) to the findings, AFs, compound chains, and business impacts that aggregate under it. The renderer (scripts/compose_threat_model.py) emits one bullet per non-empty class in fixed order; classes with no findings are skipped, NOT emitted as empty placeholders.

schema_version.description: Bump only on breaking changes to this schema.

actors.description: Threat actors present in this assessment, in display order. Slugs MUST exist as keys in data/posture-actor-labels.yaml. The renderer emits one card per entry in the ACTORS subgraph of the heatmap, looks up the label and default_subtitle from the labels file, and re-orders cards according to the labels file's `order:` list.

attack_paths.description: One entry per non-empty attack class. Order MUST match the order of the corresponding classes in data/attack-class-taxonomy.yaml — the renderer assigns glyphs ① ② … sequentially to entries in this list. An assessment with zero injection findings simply omits the `injection` entry; the next class in the file gets ①, and so on.

class.description: Slug of one canonical class in data/attack-class-taxonomy.yaml. Each slug appears at most once across the array.

actor.description: The threat actor that initiates this attack class (or, for victim-targeting classes like XSS/CSRF, the actor that is *targeted by* the arrow). Must be present in the top-level `actors` array.

target.description: Which side of the diagram the attack arrow lands on. `client` / `application` / `data` correspond to the three Tier subgraph members; `victim` means the arrow points at the Shop User actor card on the left.

description.description: ONE generic sentence describing the class as a whole — NOT a per-vector walkthrough. The renderer concatenates this with the actor + target into the bullet header. Keep it CWE-cluster-level, not finding-specific.

architectural_root_causes.description: Architectural-finding ids that this attack class aggregates under. Each AF must exist in the threat model's architectural_findings section.

findings.description: Concrete F-NNN findings that belong to this class. Required: at least 1 — empty classes must be omitted from the array entirely (not emitted with zero findings).

attack_chains.description: Compound-chain ids that materialise this attack class. Use the canonical CC-NN slug (`cc-01`, `cc-02`, …) — same anchor as the §8.F Compound Attack Chains headers and the §3.1 walkthroughs. The renderer emits a sub-bullet only when this list is non-empty.

impact.description: Business-impact slugs from data/business-impact-taxonomy.yaml. Rendered as a comma-separated `Impact:` line. Order matters — the most likely / highest-severity impact comes first.
